title: veronica longifolia subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Veronica longifolia subsp. septentrionalis, also known as the long-leaved speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory problems, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Veronica longifolia subsp. septentrionalis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
